deduplication_summary.note: Input contains duplicate hypothesis_ids in the TECH2 batch (TECH2-H003 through TECH2-H008 each appear twice with different content). Group descriptions clarify which instance is referenced.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MorphoBlueConnector._getPositionTVL adds borrowAmount instead of subtracting it, inflating vault TVL by ~2x the borrow value (plus double-counting via held tokens)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same arithmetic error in MorphoBlueConnector._getPositionTVL where borrowAmount is added instead of subtracted. ECON-H007 describes a downstream performance fee extraction scenario but the root cause is identical. ECON-H016 and TECH2-H001 restate the same bug. OPS-H001, ECON2-H001, and OPS2-H001 are cross-generator duplicates. TECH-H001 selected for most comprehensive analysis covering both the formula error and the double-counting via _updateTokenInRegistry.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Registry onlyVaultMaintainer modifier uses || instead of && in revert condition, requiring BOTH maintainer AND EMERGENCY_ROLE instead of EITHER
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same boolean logic error in Registry.onlyVaultMaintainer modifier. TECH2-H004 here is 'Registry onlyVaultMaintainer Modifier Locks Out Maintainer' (first instance of that ID). OPS-H019 frames it as an operational incident response concern but targets the same code defect. TECH-H002 selected for most detailed De Morgan's law analysis and comparison with correct modifiers.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Registry swap-and-pop position removal corrupts isPositionUsed mapping by recomputing key with calculatorConnector instead of original msg.sender
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same key mismatch bug in Registry.updateHoldingPosition's swap-and-pop removal logic. TECH2-H005 includes both the first instance ('Registry Position Removal Corrupts isPositionUsed') and the second instance ('Registry holding position removal corrupts isPositionUsed') — both target the identical code path. Both instances of TECH2-H006 with registry-related content also target this same root cause. TECH-H003 selected for clearest root cause explanation with code flow analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AerodromeConnector._getPositionTVL ignores gauge-staked LP tokens, counting only IERC20(pool).balanceOf and missing staked balance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same accounting gap where AerodromeConnector._getPositionTVL only counts unstaked LP balance, missing gauge-staked tokens. TECH2-H006 here is 'Aerodrome Staked LP Tokens Excluded from TVL' (first instance). ECON-H019 is a restated version from same specialist. ECON-H004 selected for most comprehensive economic impact analysis including stake/unstake manipulation scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: resetMiddle for withdraw queue does not reset currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmount, causing double-counting when requests are recalculated
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same state inconsistency where resetMiddle moves withdrawQueue.middle backwards without resetting totalCBAmount, causing additive double-counting on recalculation. TECH-H005 selected for precise line references and clear technical description of the accumulation mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Withdraw group state machine allows calculateWithdrawShares during fulfilled-but-not-executed state (isStarted=true, isFullfilled=true), orphaning accumulated totalCBAmount when executeWithdraw deletes the group
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same state machine bug where the guard in calculateWithdrawShares only blocks (isStarted=true, isFullfilled=false) but allows (true, true), leading to orphaned calculations destroyed by delete currentWithdrawGroup. TECH2-H008 here is 'Withdraw Group State Machine Allows Orphaned Calculations' (first instance). TECH2-H004 here is 'Withdrawal group accounting can be corrupted during fulfilled-but-not-cleared state' (second instance of that ID). ECON-H013 escalates severity but describes the same root mechanism. TECH-H006 selected for most precise state machine analysis with exact guard conditions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: UNIv3Connector._getPositionTVL uses aggregate pool position key (positionManager, tickLower, tickUpper) instead of specific NFT tokenId, overstating TVL
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same issue where UNIv3Connector uses pool.positions(key) with the NonfungiblePositionManager as owner, returning aggregate liquidity for all NFTs at the same tick range instead of the connector's specific position. TECH-H007 selected for most detailed explanation of the Uniswap V3 position key mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: collectManagementFees underflows when preformanceFeeSharesWaitingForDistribution (unminted) plus fee receiver balances exceeds totalSupply
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same arithmetic underflow in collectManagementFees where currentFeeShares includes unminted preformanceFeeSharesWaitingForDistribution, causing (totalShares - currentFeeShares) to revert. TECH2-H007 here is 'Management Fee Collection Reverts Due to Underflow' (first instance). ECON-H022 frames it differently but targets the same subtraction. TECH-H008 selected for most precise description of the underflow mechanism and preconditions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: UniswapValueOracle.getValue silently truncates uint256 amount to uint128, producing incorrect valuations for large token amounts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same silent uint256-to-uint128 downcast truncation in UniswapValueOracle.getValue. TECH-H009 selected for clear and concise technical analysis noting that Solidity 0.8.x checked arithmetic does not catch downcasting.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ChainlinkOracleConnector does not normalize output using feed.decimals(), causing systematic price misquoting for cross-decimal token pairs
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing feed decimals normalization in ChainlinkOracleConnector.getValueFromChainlinkFeed. TECH2-H003 here is 'Chainlink Oracle Missing Decimal Normalization Inflates TVL' (first instance of that ID). TECH-H017 is from the same specialist but focuses on config-dependency. ECON-H002 selected for most comprehensive analysis with concrete 100x overvaluation example (WETH→USDC with 8-decimal feed).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Flash loan address in onlyManager modifier grants global manager access across all vaults and connectors, enabling privilege escalation if set to a public flash loan provider
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same privilege escalation via registry.flashLoan() being included in the onlyManager modifier, granting a single global address full manager access. ECON-H021 is a restated version from the same specialist. ECON-H009 selected for most comprehensive attack economics, including cross-vault impact assessment and deployment evidence analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is an empty function (no-op), completely bypassing the intended withdrawal verification security mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing implementation of Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity which is empty, providing no verification of connector withdrawal amounts. ECON2-H004 selected for most complete attack chain description including downstream fund extraction path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unrestricted burnShares function allows users to burn shares locked by pending withdrawal requests, potentially bricking withdrawal execution
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same issue where public burnShares() can desync withdrawRequestsByAddress and actual balances. TECH2-H003 here is 'Unprivileged share burn enables grief/denial of withdrawals' (second instance of that ID, NOT the Chainlink one). Selected for most detailed analysis of the _update interaction and specific executeWithdraw underflow scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: NoyaValueOracle multi-hop routing passes original asset instead of intermediate quotingToken in recursive call, breaking routes with 2+ hops
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same bug where _getValue loop calls _getValue(asset, sources[i], initialValue) instead of _getValue(quotingToken, sources[i], initialValue), breaking multi-hop price conversions. TECH-H018 selected for more precise code-level description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AccountingManager.sendTokensToTrustedAddress allows any active connector to drain arbitrary tokens without per-operation authorization or accounting checks
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same unrestricted pull-based token transfer from AccountingManager to any enabled connector via sendTokensToTrustedAddress. TECH2-H005 here is 'Unbounded token extraction from AccountingManager' (second instance of that ID). TECH-H014 selected for clearest description of the vulnerability and its config-dependency.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Public checkIfTVLHasDroped allows anyone to reset pending performance fees during temporary TVL dips, enabling griefing of strategy managers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same game theory vulnerability where the public checkIfTVLHasDroped function can be called during temporary market dips to wipe out pending performance fees. ECON-H023 selected for more detailed analysis including oracle manipulation vector.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Deposit TVL check runs after baseToken transfer but before totalAWFDeposit update, causing inflated TVL and premature deposit limit rejection
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same ordering issue in AccountingManager.deposit where safeTransferFrom precedes the TVL() check while totalAWFDeposit is incremented after, temporarily inflating TVL. TECH2-H007 here is 'Deposit limit check uses TVL() after transferFrom' (second instance of that ID). Selected for more precise mechanism description.
